subject: Snapshot/live-frame buffering guard + consume broker snapshot frame
context: web-audit flags a race in src/lib/term/xterm-client.ts: connect() resolves before the socket is truly open, so live WS frames can interleave with the snapshot write, garbling scrollback on (re)attach and on rapid tab switch. Fix = buffer live frames until the snapshot has been written, then flush in order. New sequence: open WS (buffering mode ON) -> receive/fetch snapshot -> write snapshot to xterm -> flush buffered frames in arrival order -> go live (buffering OFF). With task_002 shipped, the FIRST frame the socket receives is now the broker's authoritative `snapshot` frame (@ddx/term-contract SnapshotFrame) — consume it: on a 'snapshot' frame, xterm.restoreSnapshot(decodedData) then flush the buffer. Still keep a fallback to the REST GET /api/v1/terminals/:id/snapshot path in case the broker snapshot frame is absent (older broker), but prefer the frame. tmux control-mode escapes (\033/\015) must already be raw by the time restoreSnapshot runs — the broker decodes them (task_002); verify the web side does not double-decode. Guard the stale-client path: a disposed client's onReconnect / buffered-flush must NOT fire after dispose() — the this.disposed net exists; extend it to cover the buffered-flush path. Do NOT rewrite the reconnect-backoff (solid, jittered exponential capped at 60). Align the route-driven mount in view/[terminalId]/page.tsx (task_003) with this buffering contract. Add an xterm-client.spec.ts test proving buffer-until-painted ordering and that a snapshot frame paints before buffered live frames.
steps: Read web-audit.md + xterm-client.ts to map the current connect()/onmessage/restoreSnapshot lifecycle and the this.disposed guard.
Add a frame buffer (private pending array + a `painted` boolean). While not painted, push incoming server frames into the buffer instead of writing them to xterm.
On receiving a 'snapshot' frame (or the REST snapshot fallback), write it via restoreSnapshot, set painted=true, then flush the buffer in FIFO order and clear it; subsequent frames write live.
Ensure the buffered-flush and any onReconnect early-return when this.disposed is true (extend the existing guard to the new flush path).

Update view/[terminalId]/page.tsx switch/mount ordering to match: open (buffering) -> snapshot frame or REST -> flush -> live; drop the redundant separate REST fetch when the broker snapshot frame arrives (keep as fallback).

Add xterm-client.spec.ts cases: (a) live frames arriving before snapshot are buffered and flushed after, in order; (b) a snapshot frame paints before buffered live frames; (c) a flush after dispose() is a no-op.
pitfalls: FM2: if the buffer guard is missing under the new route-driven mount, a fast broker paints live frames before the snapshot -> garbled scrollback on every deep-link. Buffer-until-painted is mandatory.
Do not double-decode tmux escapes — the broker (task_002) already decodes \033/\015 to raw bytes; the web side passes them straight to restoreSnapshot.
A disposed client must not flush or reconnect — extend this.disposed coverage to the flush path (stale second client writing to the wrong terminal is the broker's 1008-close case).
Do NOT rewrite reconnect-backoff (WS3 NOT line) and do NOT change the @ddx/term-contract frame schema here (it changed in task_001).
validation: pnpm --filter ddx-term-web typecheck && pnpm --filter ddx-term-web lint && pnpm --filter ddx-term-web test
acceptance_criteria: WHEN live frames arrive before the snapshot is painted THE SYSTEM SHALL buffer them and flush in order after the snapshot writes (AC3.1, AC3.2).

WHEN a broker snapshot frame is received on cold attach THE SYSTEM SHALL paint it as the first authoritative frame without a separate REST call (AC4.2), falling back to REST only if the frame is absent.
WHEN tabs are switched rapidly THE SYSTEM SHALL show each terminal's own correct last output with no out-of-order paint (AC3.3).
WHEN a flush is attempted after dispose() THE SYSTEM SHALL perform no write.
